topic: Two-pole geomagnetic model — verification of EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1243 surface-aperture defender pivot foreclosure
our_argument_summary: WIN-053 (Two-pole geomagnetic model). Verdict: Self-Contradicted. We argue the dome's toroidal architecture is topologically a ring magnet, in which ∇·B=0 forces a ~1,600:1 magnitude asymmetry between source and return — yet the dome's fitted B(r) gives near-equal coefficients A=62,376 nT and C=64,852 nT at both ends, a 1,660:1 self-contradiction within the dome's own geometry. Independently the exponential B ∝ e^(−r/λ) form is justified post-hoc rather than derived from the toroidal architecture; A, C, and λ are observed pole values relabeled (no derivation from disc_radius, firmament_height, or moon_altitude); λ is the same parameter pressed into service for an unrelated phenomenon (firmament height H(r)). c11 verifies EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1243: a new ~270-word paragraph forecloses the surface-aperture re-interpretation defender pivot (C describes a south surface aperture, not a sub-terranean return density).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py contains no domain or branch for WIN-053. No script computes B(r) from disc/firmament parameters. The 20% RMS improvement claim is asserted on the dome site, not produced by repository code.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — no automation exists for WIN-053 in the dome's repository.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. detail_evidence states 'monitor.py does not track WIN-053, and no script computes B(r)' — confirmed via grep against current raw-text/monitor.py this run.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no WIN-053 domain or branch; no B(r) computation, no field-strength comparison, no observatory data fetch tied to this WIN. Grep against raw-text/monitor.py this run returned 0 matches for the WIN's parameter set.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: WMM2025 / IGRF-13 / CHAOS-7 already model the geomagnetic field at all latitudes including both polar regions, with degree-13 spherical harmonics achieving ~50 nT global RMS. The dome's ~10,000–13,000 nT RMS (20% of typical field strength) is two orders of magnitude worse. The dome's contribution is renaming the mechanism (geodynamo → toroidal-aetheric flow) while adopting observed pole magnitudes as fit parameters.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Pole-region field strengths (~55,000 nT) have been measured continuously since the 19th century and tabulated in IGRF since 1968; A=62,376 and C=64,852 are post-hoc fit coefficients to those measurements, not pre-data predictions. PRED-TOROID-002 'confirmation' date 2026-03-23 precedes its registration date 2026-04-04 per detail_extra — registered-after-the-fact retrodiction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation of A, C, or λ from disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, or moon_altitude=2,534 km exists on the dome site or in monitor.py. λ=8,619 km is also used as the H(r) scale length, but that is parameter sharing across unrelated phenomena, not derivation.

patch_verification.summary: EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1243 patch landed cleanly. The new ~270-word paragraph addressing the surface-aperture re-interpretation defender pivot is present in WIN-053.detail_evidence between the close of the narrow-channel-return rescue paragraph and the start of the functional-form-contradiction paragraph. Verification pattern 'fourth charitable reading drops the sub-terranean' matches. WMM2025 and IGRF-13 NCEI URLs match the URL conventions used elsewhere in the WIN. detail_evidence character length grew from 8,936 (c10) to 11,307 (c11), consistent with the EXP's declared replacement_word_count of 276 (net add 260 words ≈ ~2,200 chars including HTML tagging — observed ~2,371 char delta is in expected range).

patches.description: Insert ~270-word paragraph forecloseing the surface-aperture defender pivot (C describes a south surface aperture, not a sub-terranean return density). Kernel-of-truth acknowledged head-on; two structural rebuttals: (i) symmetric flux conservation requires A=C exactly; (ii) both coefficients carry positive signs in B(r), so the dome treats both apertures as flux sources rather than a source-and-sink pair.
patches.verbatim_text_present: A fourth charitable reading drops the sub-terranean framing entirely: perhaps C = 64,852 nT describes a second surface aperture
patches.notes:
Anchor uniqueness preserved — the find-text 'not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific toroidal architecture.

A second self-contradiction' appears exactly once in current wins.json (confirmed via grep). New paragraph sits between the narrow-channel rescue closure and the functional-form contradiction paragraph as declared in the EXP. No existing cross-references (§6.1, §6.4, WIN-012, INTERMAGNET, CHAOS-7, South Atlantic Anomaly) were disturbed.

patches.description: tldr_evidence previously contained duplicated em-dashed lead-in clause ('Both coefficients were fitted to observed WMM pole strengths — Both coefficients were fitted to observed pole-region field strengths —'). c10 H0 flagged as moderate editorial defect.
patches.verbatim_text_present: Both coefficients were fitted to observed pole-region field strengths: A=62,376 nT is ∼13% above the geographic north pole
patches.notes: Duplication removed; clause replaced with single em-dash-to-colon form. tldr_evidence length dropped from 721 (c10) to 657 (c11), consistent with the ~60-char duplication removal. Verified: tldr no longer contains the substring 'observed WMM pole strengths'.

holes_found.description: EXP-377 paragraph compresses the symmetric/asymmetric dichotomy. The (i) rebuttal opens with 'Symmetric flux conservation requires A = C exactly (two apertures of equal area must produce equal surface fields...)' and then immediately observes the 4% asymmetry, transitioning to 'either the coefficients are tuned to globe observations (a curve fit, not a derivation from any symmetric dome geometry) or the dome is implicitly borrowing the asymmetric-geodynamo physics.' The logic is correct but slightly compressed for a non-expert reader: the implicit dichotomy is (a) if the dome geometry is symmetric, B_N = B_S exactly and the 4% asymmetry is contradiction; (b) if the dome geometry is asymmetric (4% area difference), the dome must derive that 4% from a published parameter, which it does not — so it is a curve-fit. The EXP's defender_pivots_addressed array #2 has the cleaner version ('the dome would need to publish an INDEPENDENT derivation of the aperture-area ratio') but the published-derivation-demand is not lifted into the prose. A reader pivoting to 'maybe the apertures aren't exactly equal area' is partially answered but not airtight in the paragraph itself.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional one-sentence tightening at the end of (i): add 'The dome publishes no aperture-area ratio in its parameters; the only published surface circuit is the symmetric one bounded by disc_radius=20,015 km, so a 4% area asymmetry would require an as-yet-undisclosed independent parameter, not the post-hoc fit currently presented.' Minor severity — the existing prose handles the pivot, this would make it explicit.

holes_found.description: Sign-convention rebuttal (ii) leans entirely on the prior paragraph's directional argument. The (ii) rebuttal asserts 'Both coefficients carry positive signs in B(r), so the dome treats both apertures as flux sources rather than a source-and-sink pair. In any closed magnetic circuit one aperture must be a sink — the directional sign contradiction documented above is unchanged by re-interpreting C as a second surface aperture.' This references the earlier paragraph's note that 'B(r)'s positive coefficient C = 64,852 nT assigns the same outward sign at both ends.' A defender's strongest pivot here is to claim B(r) is a magnitude expression (always positive by construction) and that the toroidal flow direction handles signs separately — i.e., positivity of A and C is a notation choice, not a topology claim. The EXP's defender_pivots_addressed #3 has the rebuttal (both observed surface fields cannot simultaneously point outward at the geographic poles, since real-pole inclination data disagrees), but that physical rebuttal is not in the integrated paragraph. The current text is correct but its load-bearing relies on a defender accepting the prior paragraph's signed-component reading.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional one-sentence tightening at the end of (ii): explicitly note that the dome fits POSITIVE values to MAGNITUDES of OBSERVED surface fields, but real-pole inclination at the geographic poles points oppositely (inward at one, outward at the other) — so a magnitude-only re-interpretation would still need to specify which aperture is the sink, which the dome's B(r) does not. Minor severity — the underlying argument is sound, this would close the rescue route explicitly rather than by reference.

holes_found.description: Editorial numbering inconsistency. The detail_evidence rescue sequence now reads (1) the unlabeled main 'A charitable reading...' (narrow-channel return), with internal 'First' and 'Second' sub-points; (2) 'A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric'; (3) 'A fourth charitable reading drops the sub-terranean framing entirely:' (the new EXP-377 paragraph). The 'third'/'fourth' labels are consistent with the prior prose's counting (which treated the 'First'/'Second' sub-points of rescue 1 as occupying slots 1 and 2), but a fresh reader will look for 'second' and 'third' main rescue labels and not find them. The labeling reads naturally enough that it is not a factual error, but a casual reader could be momentarily confused.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: renumber to 'A second charitable reading...' / 'A third retreat...' / 'A fourth charitable reading...' would not be wrong, but the cleanest fix is to relabel 'third retreat' → 'A second retreat' and 'fourth charitable reading' → 'A third charitable reading.' Minor editorial; do not block on this. Alternatively leave as-is — the numbering tracks the prose's own narrative of rescue attempts and is internally consistent.

holes_found.description: ISS-1245 (c5 H7, flux direction polarity / 'topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole') remains open and is only PARTIALLY addressed by EXP-377's (ii) sub-argument. The EXP-377 paragraph notes that 'both coefficients carry positive signs in B(r), so the dome treats both apertures as flux sources rather than a source-and-sink pair' — which captures the source-vs-sink topology concern. But ISS-1245's specific suggested wording adds the framing that the dome's formula is 'topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole' — a topology-mismatch label that is not yet present in the prose. ISS-1245 stays open in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json (status assigned-analyst, severity minor).
holes_found.recommendation: ISS-1245 is still on the analyst expansion queue. The decider may consider whether the existing (ii) text is sufficient and close ISS-1245 as 'covered by EXP-377', or route to analyst for a one-sentence 'topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole' addition. Recommend the latter — the topology-label framing is a punchy reader-facing summary that the current prose lacks. No new action required by curmudgeon.

stronger_arguments.description: The aperture-area derivation demand could be tightened by citing the dome's own claim in detail_extra that the Sub-H(r) δ = 6,371 km parameter is 'a globe-derived constant with no derivation from dome geometry.' If the dome already imports globe constants for sub-terranean depth, the absence of any aperture-area parameter (and the 4% asymmetry's tracking of WMM/IGRF) cleanly reads as another globe-import: the asymmetric geodynamo's pole-strength signature was inherited along with δ. This sharpens the 'borrowing geodynamo physics' rebuttal in (i) with a parallel structural failure already documented in the same WIN.

kernel_of_truth.description: The author of ISS-1243's H3 critique was correct that the surface-aperture re-interpretation was a live defender pivot not foreclosed by the c10-era detail_evidence. The c10 prose explicitly addressed (a) the narrow-channel sub-terranean return rescue and (b) the aetheric-vs-Maxwellian 'flux conservation does not apply' rescue, but left the surface-aperture re-interpretation (C describes a second surface aperture at the Antarctic re-entry, not the sub-terranean return density) as an open rescue route — see data/wins.json:WIN-053.detail_evidence pre-EXP-377 (the prior 'third retreat' paragraph closes the aetheric rescue but does not address surface-aperture re-interpretation). EXP-377 closes that gap with an explicit kernel-of-truth acknowledgment: 'a closed two-aperture circuit demands similar field magnitudes at both ends, and the dome's fitted A and C are similar (~62,000–65,000 nT)' (data/wins.json:WIN-053.detail_evidence, EXP-377 paragraph, kernel-acknowledgment sentence).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Acknowledging the kernel does not rescue the dome because the structural argument operates one level above the surface/sub-terranean choice. Symmetric flux conservation requires A = C exactly for equal-area apertures (data/wins.json:WIN-053.detail_evidence EXP-377 paragraph, (i) sub-argument), but the dome's fitted A=62,376 and C=64,852 differ by ~4% and that 4% tracks WMM2025/IGRF-13 pole asymmetry to within fitting noise (cross-cited https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model and https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/international-geomagnetic-reference-field per EXP-377 patch text). Both coefficients carry positive signs in B(r), so the dome treats both apertures as flux sources rather than a source-and-sink pair, contradicting the source-sink topology any closed magnetic circuit demands (data/wins.json:WIN-053.detail_evidence EXP-377 paragraph, (ii) sub-argument). Re-interpreting C as a second surface aperture relocates the contradictions; it does not eliminate them. The 1,660:1 magnitude self-contradiction documented in the lead paragraph is unchanged (data/wins.json:WIN-053.detail_evidence, flux-conservation lead paragraph).

deeper_analysis:
EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1243 verification: the patch landed cleanly and closes ISS-1243 substantively. The new paragraph acknowledges a real kernel of truth (closed two-aperture circuits demand similar field magnitudes) before showing why the dome's specific fitted values fail that requirement. The two structural rebuttals (symmetric flux conservation requires A=C exactly; both coefficients positive-signed contradicts source-sink topology) are correct and self-consistent with the rest of WIN-053.detail_evidence.

The three new minor holes (compressed dichotomy in (i), magnitude-vs-vector pivot risk in (ii), editorial numbering) are polishing items — none rises to a level that would change the verdict or threaten the argument's load-bearing structure. The aperture-area-derivation demand and the magnitude-only rescue rebuttal are both present in the EXP's defender_pivots_addressed array but were compressed out of the integrated prose; if the analyst chooses to lift those into the paragraph in a future iteration, the rebuttal becomes airtight rather than reliant on charitable reader interpolation. The editorial numbering ('third retreat'/'fourth charitable reading') is a side-effect of the c5-era prose structure and is harmless if left alone.

Advocate-mode (other side): a dome defender's strongest pivot against the new paragraph is to grant the source-sink topology but claim the dome's published B(r) is intentionally a magnitude-only fit — i.e., the dome AGREES that one aperture is a sink, and the positive coefficient C just reflects that |B| at the south aperture is what gets measured. This pivot collapses on closer examination because (a) the dome's own RMS-improvement claim is computed against directional surface-observatory data (INTERMAGNET, CHAOS-7), not against |B| only, and (b) at the geographic poles real-world inclination data has opposite radial signs (field points into Earth at one, out at the other), so a magnitude-only model is structurally incapable of reproducing the directional data the dome cites for its 20% RMS metric. This argument is implicit in the existing detail_evidence but not laid out at this level of detail anywhere — a future cycle could explicitly add it. Not a blocker for c11.

The WIN's verdict (Self-Contradicted) and core load-bearing argument (∇·B=0 flux conservation in the dome's own claimed ring-magnet topology yields a ~1,660:1 magnitude contradiction with the fitted B(r)) are unchanged and untouched by EXP-377. c11 confirms the verdict holds.

Unresolved_prior_cycle: only ISS-1245 remains open from the c10 list (was c5 H7). ISS-1244, 1246, 1305, 1307, 1446, 1447 all closed since c10 (confirmed via closed-issues.json grep this run). ISS-1245 is partially covered by EXP-377's (ii) but the specific 'topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole' framing is not yet in the prose.

Cross-WIN consistency check: WIN-053's verdict text references WIN-012's parallel κ-coupling collapse. WIN-012 verdict is Self-Contradicted (confirmed via wins.json this run). The cross-reference is sound and supports the broader 'parameter-sharing-without-derivation' failure mode that both WINs document.

IGRF-13 URL (https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/international-geomagnetic-reference-field) — matches URL used elsewhere in WIN-053.detail_evidence; resolves to active NCEI/NOAA IGRF product page (URL convention consistent with rest of document)

citations_unchecked: Centered-dipole calculation B(θ≈170°) ≈ 60,700 nT — inherited from c8/c10 verification; not re-derived this run

summary_for_decider: EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1243 patch verified — verdict (Self-Contradicted) holds, confidence 0.88. The new ~270-word surface-aperture-rescue paragraph closes ISS-1243 substantively with a kernel-of-truth acknowledgment plus two structural rebuttals. Three minor polishing holes flagged: (a) the symmetric/asymmetric dichotomy in rebuttal (i) is slightly compressed (defender pivot 'maybe apertures aren't equal area' is implicitly answered but not airtight); (b) the magnitude-vs-vector pivot in (ii) leans on the prior paragraph's directional argument and would benefit from an explicit 'real-pole-inclination disagrees with magnitude-only fit' sentence; (c) editorial numbering ('third retreat'/'fourth charitable reading') is internally consistent but momentarily confusing. None rises to a major/critical finding. Recommended actions: (1) Close ISS-1243 as fixed (verification grep succeeds: 'fourth charitable reading drops the sub-terranean' present in wins.json). (2) Decide whether to route the three minor polish items to analyst for a small EXP, or accept as-is — argument is sound either way. (3) ISS-1245 (c5 H7 flux direction polarity) is partially covered by EXP-377's (ii) but the specific 'topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole' framing is not yet in the prose — decider may close ISS-1245 as 'covered by EXP-377' or route for the topology-label addition. (4) Verify TLDR duplication artifact (c10 H0) is also resolved — current tldr_evidence shows the colon form, no duplication, length dropped from 721 to 657 chars. No carry-over patches stranded. queue_id 356 marked here.
